verification.target_vocab_notes: 分かる appears clearly in the sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Contains だろう which is plain volitional/probability form (N4 grammar). Also uses quotation と which with nominalization (彼女に会うのは) creates complex embedded clause structure.
verification.difficulty_notes: N4 level. Uses だろう and complex embedding with と quotation particle.
verification.overall_notes: Grammar exceeds N5 with だろう form and complex quotation structure

verification.target_vocab_notes: 分かる appears in plain form わかる
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, が particle, dictionary form. Basic N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Lower-mid N5. Simple statement with が particle pattern. Straightforward.
verification.overall_notes: Excellent simple N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 言う appears in conditional form いえば
verification.grammar_notes: Contains 'ば' conditional form which is N4 grammar. The sentence structure 'if you say X frankly...' uses conditional.
verification.difficulty_notes: N4 level. Uses conditional ば form which exceeds N5.
verification.overall_notes: Grammar exceeds N5 with ば conditional form

verification.target_vocab_notes: 言う appears in passive form 言われる
verification.grammar_notes: Contains passive form 言われる (is said/told) which is N4 grammar. Also uses と conditional particle.
verification.difficulty_notes: N4 level. Uses passive form and conditional と.
verification.overall_notes: Grammar exceeds N5 with passive form 言われる and conditional と

verification.target_vocab_notes: 言う appears in quotation pattern という
verification.grammar_notes: Uses という quotation pattern with の nominalization (というのが). While という is borderline, this basic usage of defining/naming is sometimes included in late N5. However, it's complex.
verification.difficulty_notes: Upper N5/borderline N4. Uses という quotation pattern with nominalization and が particle. Complex structure.
verification.overall_notes: という quotation pattern is borderline N4. Complex nominalization structure. Conservative approach would reject.

verification.target_vocab_notes: 言う appears in という pattern
verification.grammar_notes: Duplicate of example 2. Same complex という structure.
verification.difficulty_notes: Same as example 2.
verification.overall_notes: Duplicate of example 2. Borderline complex という pattern.

verification.target_vocab_notes: 言う appears in quotation pattern だという
verification.grammar_notes: Contains という quotation pattern followed by ようだ evidential (seems/apparently). Both patterns are N4 level.
verification.difficulty_notes: N4 level. Uses quotation という plus evidential ようだ. Far too complex for N5.
verification.overall_notes: Grammar exceeds N5 with という quotation and ようだ evidential

verification.target_vocab_notes: 言う appears in quotation pattern という
verification.grammar_notes: Uses という quotation pattern with の nominalization and かしら (feminine wondering particle). The という is complex but かしら is borderline N5/N4.
verification.difficulty_notes: Upper N5/borderline. Uses という quotation, nominalization, and かしら wondering. Complex.
verification.overall_notes: という is borderline N4, かしら is also borderline. Complex structure overall.

verification.target_vocab_notes: 飲む appears in negative conditional form 飲まなければ
verification.grammar_notes: Contains なければいけません obligation pattern (must/have to) which is N4 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: N4 level. Uses obligation pattern which exceeds N5.
verification.overall_notes: Grammar exceeds N5 with なければいけません obligation form

verification.target_vocab_notes: 飲む appears in ～に行く purpose construction (飲みに行く - go to drink)
verification.grammar_notes: Uses masu-stem + に行く pattern to express purpose (go to do X). This is N5 level.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid N5. Uses purpose construction with に行く. Standard N5 pattern.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with purpose construction

verification.target_vocab_notes: 飲む appears in たい desire form 飲みたい
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, が particle with たい desire form, and sentence-ending な. All N5 level.
verification.difficulty_notes: Lower-mid N5. Simple desire statement with が~たい pattern. Straightforward.
verification.overall_notes: Excellent N5 example with desire form

verification.target_vocab_notes: 練習 appears in the sentence (though target shows れんしゅうする as verb form)
verification.grammar_notes: Uses 毎日, ある existence verb, のみ particle (only - formal/emphatic), and よ particle. The のみ is more formal/literary but the basic structure is N5.
verification.difficulty_notes: Upper N5. Uses のみ for 'only' which is more formal than だけ. Slightly literary but manageable.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with slightly formal のみ particle

verification.target_vocab_notes: 何 appears as question word 何を (what)
verification.grammar_notes: Uses に particle, question word 何, を particle, ています for possession state, question marker か. All N5 level.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid N5. Question with ています for state of possession. Standard complexity.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with question word

verification.target_vocab_notes: 何 appears as counter question 何人 (how many people)
verification.grammar_notes: Uses には particle combination, counter 何人, が particle, います. All N5 level.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid N5. Uses counter with question word, requires understanding of counters.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with counter usage

verification.target_vocab_notes: 何 appears in counter 何人
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, counter 何人, が particle, います. All N5 level.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid N5. Similar to example 1, uses counter with question word.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 厚い appears clearly, though note the meaning listed is 'kind' but the sentence uses it as 'thick'
verification.grammar_notes: Uses demonstrative この, は particle, とても adverb, i-adjective. Basic N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Low N5. Simple adjective statement. Very straightforward.
verification.overall_notes: Excellent simple N5 example. Note: target meaning shows 'kind' but sentence uses 'thick' meaning.

verification.target_vocab_notes: 厚い appears as adjective modifier
verification.grammar_notes: Contains てた which is contracted past progressive (reading + was reading = 読んでた from 読んでいた). This is N4 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: N4 level. Uses contracted past progressive てた.
verification.overall_notes: Grammar exceeds N5 with past progressive てた (contracted from ていた). Also note meaning mismatch.

verification.target_vocab_notes: 厚い appears in te-form 厚くて
verification.grammar_notes: Uses demonstrative この, は particle, te-form to connect adjectives (厚くて - thick and), i-adjective 重い, copula です. All N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid N5. Uses te-form to connect two adjectives. Standard pattern.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with adjective connection. Note meaning issue with target.

verification.target_vocab_notes: 毎月 appears as 毎月 (listed as まいつき in kana)
verification.grammar_notes: Uses demonstrative この, は particle, time expression 毎月, and dictionary form 出る. All N5 level.
verification.difficulty_notes: Lower-mid N5. Simple time expression with basic verb. Straightforward.
verification.overall_notes: Excellent simple N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 閉まる appears clearly
verification.grammar_notes: Uses が particle, question word いつ, embedded question with か, and verb 分かります. Embedded question structure is complex but uses only N5 elements.
verification.difficulty_notes: Upper N5. Uses embedded question clause (いつ閉まるか). Complex but technically N5.
verification.overall_notes: Grammar is N5 but embedded question structure is quite complex for beginners.

verification.target_vocab_notes: 閉まる appears in the sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses temporal expression ~前に (before), te-form with directional いく contracted to っといで (行っておいで). While っといで is casual contracted form, the basic ていく pattern is borderline N5.
verification.difficulty_notes: Upper N5/borderline. Uses contracted casual form っといで and directional auxiliary. Complex.
verification.overall_notes: ていく directional pattern is borderline N4. Casual contraction adds complexity.

verification.target_vocab_notes: 隣 appears with の particle (隣の家 - neighboring house)
verification.grammar_notes: Uses の possessive, が particle, and copula です. Basic N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Lower-mid N5. Simple statement with possessive particle. Straightforward.
verification.overall_notes: Excellent simple N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 隣 appears with に particle (隣に - next to)
verification.grammar_notes: Uses の possessive, に particle for location, を particle, and past tense 建てた. All N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid N5. Uses location particle and past tense. Standard complexity.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 隣 appears at sentence end
verification.grammar_notes: Uses の possessive, は particle, が particle, and copula です. Standard N5 X は Y が Z pattern.
verification.difficulty_notes: Lower-mid N5. Simple statement with double particle pattern. Standard.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 登る appears in past tense form 登った
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, に particle, past tense, question marker か. All N5 level.
verification.difficulty_notes: Lower-mid N5. Simple past tense question. Straightforward.
verification.overall_notes: Excellent simple N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 登る appears in dictionary form
verification.grammar_notes: Contains 'なぜなら~からだ' which is a formal causal pattern (because). While the individual elements are N5, this formal construction なぜなら~からだ is typically considered N4.
verification.difficulty_notes: N4 level. Uses formal causal construction なぜなら~からだ.
verification.overall_notes: Grammar exceeds N5 with formal なぜなら~からだ causal pattern

verification.target_vocab_notes: 登る appears in dictionary form
verification.grammar_notes: Uses demonstrative この, に particle, dictionary form 登る, の nominalizer, は particle, とても adverb, i-adjective. All N5 level.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid-upper N5. Uses の nominalization with embedded verb phrase. Moderately complex but N5.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with nominalization

verification.target_vocab_notes: 登る appears as のぼる in kana
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, adverb 簡単に, に particle, dictionary form. All N5 level.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid N5. Uses adverb and basic verb structure. Standard complexity.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 汚い appears clearly
verification.kana_notes: Error: 'かの' should be 'かれの' (his). The kana shows かのへや but Japanese shows 彼の部屋 which should read かれのへや.
verification.grammar_notes: Uses の possessive, は particle, i-adjective. Basic N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Lower-mid N5. Simple adjective statement with possessive.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 grammar but kana error present. 彼の should read かれの not かの.

verification.target_vocab_notes: 汚い appears as きたない in kana
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, na-adjective たいへんで (te-form connection), i-adjective きたない, explanatory のだ. While のだ is borderline, basic usage is often included in late N5.
verification.difficulty_notes: Upper N5. Uses te-form to connect adjectives and explanatory のだ. Moderately complex.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with adjective connection

verification.target_vocab_notes: 汚い appears as modifier
verification.grammar_notes: Uses demonstrative その, i-adjective modifier きたない, を particle, に particle, negative imperative 入れないで. All N5 level.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid-upper N5. Uses negative imperative and multiple particles. Standard upper N5.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 汚い appears as きたない
verification.grammar_notes: Duplicate of example 1. Same grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Same as example 1.
verification.overall_notes: Duplicate of example 1 but acceptable N5 grammar

verification.target_vocab_notes: 雨 appears clearly
verification.grammar_notes: Uses adverb にわかに (suddenly), が particle, and てきた directional auxiliary (降ってきた - started to fall/came to fall). While てくる is borderline, basic usage with weather verbs is often included in N5.
verification.difficulty_notes: Upper N5. Uses directional てきた with weather verb. Borderline but manageable.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example. てきた with weather is borderline but acceptable.

verification.target_vocab_notes: 雨 appears in the sentence
verification.kana_notes: Error: 'おりだしている' should be 'ふりだしている' (降りだしている uses 降り from 降る 'to fall/rain', not 降り 'to get off' which reads 'ori').
verification.grammar_notes: Contains その上 which is a conjunction meaning 'moreover/besides/in addition' - this is N4. Also uses ている progressive and だす auxiliary.
verification.difficulty_notes: N4 level. Uses N4 conjunction その上.
verification.overall_notes: Grammar exceeds N5 with その上 conjunction. Also has kana error.

verification.target_vocab_notes: 雨 appears clearly
verification.grammar_notes: Contains だろうか which is plain volitional/probability form with question (N4 grammar).
verification.difficulty_notes: N4 level. Uses だろうか probability questioning.
verification.overall_notes: Grammar exceeds N5 with だろうか form

verification.target_vocab_notes: 速い appears clearly
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, dictionary form verb 歩く with の nominalization (歩くの), が particle, i-adjective. All N5 level.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid-upper N5. Uses の nominalization with verb. Moderately complex but N5.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example with nominalization

verification.target_vocab_notes: 速い appears clearly
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, が particle, i-adjective. Standard N5 X は Y が Adj pattern.
verification.difficulty_notes: Lower-mid N5. Simple statement with double particle pattern. Straightforward.
verification.overall_notes: Excellent simple N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 速い appears clearly
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, dictionary form 走る with の nominalization, が particle, i-adjective. All N5 level.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid-upper N5. Same pattern as example 0 with nominalization.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 新しい appears as adjective modifier
verification.grammar_notes: Uses demonstrative あの, で particle for location, i-adjective 新しい, を particle, たい desire form. All N5 level.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid N5. Uses location particle, adjective modifier, desire form. Standard complexity.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 新しい appears as adjective modifier
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, i-adjective modifier, を particle, past tense 買った. All basic N5.
verification.difficulty_notes: Lower-mid N5. Simple past tense statement with adjective modifier.
verification.overall_notes: Excellent simple N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 新しい appears as adjective modifier
verification.grammar_notes: Uses は particle, i-adjective modifier, を particle, past tense なくした. All N5 level.
verification.difficulty_notes: Lower-mid N5. Simple past tense statement with adjective.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 廊下 appears clearly
verification.grammar_notes: Uses に particle for location, が particle, ある existence verb. Basic N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Low N5. Simple existence statement with location. Very straightforward.
verification.overall_notes: Excellent simple N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 茶色 appears clearly
verification.kana_notes: Error: 'かの' should be 'かれの' (his). The kana shows かのくつ but Japanese shows 彼の靴 which should read かれのくつ.
verification.grammar_notes: Uses の possessive, は particle, copula だ. Basic N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Low N5. Simple copula statement. Very straightforward.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 grammar but kana error. 彼の should read かれの not かの.

verification.target_vocab_notes: 茶色 appears with の particle
verification.grammar_notes: Uses の particle, が particle with 欲しい, explanatory んだ, and ではなくて (not X but) conjunction. While ではなくて is complex, it's technically N5 level negative conjunction.
verification.difficulty_notes: Upper N5. Uses んだ, が欲しい, and ではなくて negative conjunction. Complex but technically N5.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example though on complex side with ではなくて

verification.target_vocab_notes: 茶色 appears clearly
verification.grammar_notes: Uses demonstrative その, は particle, copula です. Basic N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Very low N5. Simple copula statement. Extremely straightforward.
verification.overall_notes: Perfect simple N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: コート appears clearly
verification.grammar_notes: Uses demonstrative あの, で particle for location, i-adjective, を particle, たい desire form. All N5 level.
verification.difficulty_notes: Mid N5. Uses location, adjective modifier, desire form. Standard complexity.
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: コート appears clearly
verification.grammar_notes: Uses を particle and ましょうか polite volitional question (shall I/we). Basic N5 grammar.
verification.difficulty_notes: Lower-mid N5. Polite offer/suggestion with ましょうか. Straightforward.
verification.overall_notes: Excellent N5 example with polite volitional question
